spec.reasoning: The risk flag 'data_values_match_headers' means that header names like 'Category 1', 'Agent' etc. also appear as data values in column E. Renaming these would require careful coordination across multiple locations. Instead, I chose two safer perturbations: (1) A title row insertion at the top of Sheet1, which shifts all rows down by 1 and tests whether the agent hardcodes row references like 'F22:AE29' or dynamically finds the correct data regions. The answer position would shift to F23:AE30. (2) A distractor column 'Category 10' inserted after the last column, which tests whether the agent incorrectly includes this empty/irrelevant column in its calculations. The name is plausibly similar to the existing Category 1-9 columns but clearly distinct. These perturbations change surface anchors (row positions, column count) without altering the underlying logic or difficulty of the lookup/merge task.
